fiction_catalogue.author: Srishti Jain
fiction_catalogue.bio: Srishti was born on the 9th of October 2000 to Manoj and Anita Jain. A second born in the family of Engineers and Economists, she created her own unique way of seeing the world through stories. She lived in Dombivili on the outskirts of Mumbai, Maharashtra and did her primary schooling there. Then she moved to Pune. She pursued science in her junior college but shifted to humanities to take English as her major. Though the plan changed once she stumbled across the subject of Psychology, fell in love with the human mind and behaviour, and decided to major in it instead. Sris graduated from the University of Pune with a Bachelors and Masters in Psychology and aims to be a Clinical Psychologist while she writes for the rest of her life.

books.title: Untitled (Shimla Narcotics Thriller)
books.description: The infamous drug cartel Qwan has raised its fangs again, this time operating from the city of Shimla. DSP Shrikant Pandit, of CBI Mumbai, is on route with a fresh recruit, Aniruddh Nigam, to aid the NCB where Sr Narcotics Inspector Ranjeet introduces them to the young city boy, Dhruv Saini, their chauffeur and guide. Shrikant begins in the most unusual way possible, by sightseeing and throwing a new year party. But he was failing, for there was a traitor in their circle of trust. Will Shrikant be able to catch the traitor in time or will he end up taking a decision which he will have to regret nine years later?

fiction_catalogue.author: Anita Sharma
fiction_catalogue.bio: Soft skill developer; Wellness facilitator; Author; Bilingual poet; Educator. An alumnus of Delhi University is an educator with 26 plus years of teaching experience across the country, member of the Council for Global Education, Women’s Indian Chamber of Commerce and Industry (WICCI). Hails from a family steeped in military traditions with roots in Himachal Pradesh. Runs a blog with TOI titled Random Musings and has been published by Visual Verse (Canada), Kitaab International (Singapore), Setu (Pittsburg), the Undiscovered (Om International), The Aleph Review & Indian Periodicals. Winner of House of Harmony short story competition 2025. YouTube channel for bilingual poetry- @aneetasharma27). Instagram @aneetasharma27.

books.title: Untitled (Dalhousie Thriller)
books.description: The picturesque hill station of Dalhousie is rudely jolted when a honeymooning couple chances upon a mud-caked corpse. Inspector Kanet is plunged into a high-stakes investigation, only to face constant interference by the CID based in Delhi. As secrets unravel and the race intensifies, Kanet and CID navigate a maze of deception to prevent another murder. Everyone is a suspect and there are unanswered questions—is entrepreneur Lalit Bharucha concealing a dark past while protecting his stunning wife, Kiyana? Why has the fiercely competitive Ashlesha retreated to this remote town? What’s behind Haneesha’s deceptive innocence? Who is Kabir Rana, and why is he imprisoned? With every clue exposing deeper lies and every suspect harbouring hidden agenda, this relentless thriller delivers a whirlwind of intrigue, betrayal, and revenge.

books.title: Untitled (NCR Contemporary Fiction)
books.description: Contemporary fiction set in the NCR. Four people; four lives: Each distinctive; each different. Amaira - a woman whose accident induced sleep arms her with the courage and clarity to rediscover herself. Will she succeed? Trisha - her mother’s bitter tryst with marriage colours her canvas with grey. Will she ever dabble with colours? Siddharth -straightforward and direct, his innate desire for happy endings threatens his own. Will he salvage his happiness? Abhigyan - trapped in a web of bitter relationships, he refuses to gauge how much of the rancidity is of his own making. Will he bring himself to face the writing on the wall? As they grapple with the odds that destiny has designed for them and search resolution to their internal conflicts; their lives engage and disengage. Separated by miles of age and background, the one thing that binds the four in a thread of commonality is their burning desire to take control of their journey and find a harbour of their own making. The novel touches upon class differences, domestic violence, divorce, the struggles of a lower middle-class family in a busy metro, the travails of the rich, death rites and gender bias which is deep rooted mindset in our society.

fiction_catalogue.author: Riya Menghani
fiction_catalogue.bio: Riya Menghani has done in Masters in Computers and is a working professional in the IT Industry. Since childhood, she is passionate about reading, attracted to words, be it anywhere, in books, newspaper, medicine boxes, walls, packaging materials, wherever they are spotted. She has always been a reader, a thinker, a dreamer and interested in learning with new things. Her reading habits lead her to discover the hidden writer in her. This book is her first attempt as a writer. In her free time, she loves to cook different types of dishes for her family, she loves listening to informative videos and interviews of famous personalities. She is blessed with a loving husband and 2 teenager kids, leading a happy life.

books.title: The Quest of Lost Love
books.description: This book is not a story; it’s a string of experiences that the protagonists go through. Abhishek, a college goer becomes a self-made business man, has to fight and defeat the enemies of Shagun, in order to free her from their shadows, empower her to drop her inhibitions, reunite her with her family and eventually marry her, as she is the love of his life. Shagun, a young high schooler, has to go through a lot of sufferings in life, is forced to live a life of secrecy. She is helped by her soul mate Abhishek during her journey, in order to save herself from the bad guys and to lead a normal life without any fears. Abhishek and Shagun had met during their young days and felt the first feeling of love. But got separated immediately after and lost contact with each other. Years passed as they kept waiting and searching for their first love. This story is about their journey and trials that they went through during “The Quest of Lost Love”. This book talks about the life of a girl, who goes through many ups and downs. Her main support system is often not around, leaving her to take care of her own self, when she is in vulnerable situations. Although her partner heroically pulls her out of a major hell hole, still, many times she is left struggling for a good life without any inhibitions. She is restricted by factors governing the decisions of her life, in the name of being a good girl. This book touches upon a little on the said/unsaid rules laid for a girl in some middle-class households.

fiction_catalogue.author: Shivani B Sabharwal
fiction_catalogue.bio: Shivani Sabharwal is a passionate educator, storyteller, author, and advocate for early childhood development with over 10 years of experience in shaping young minds. As the founder of Twinkling Stars by Learning Ladders, she has dedicated her career to empowering children through creative and engaging learning approaches. Shivani has authored Phonics Fun - A Journey to Sound Mastery, a comprehensive workbook series designed to make phonics fun and accessible for early learners, and Osum's Ocean Odyssey - Discovering Wonders and Friends Under the Sea, an enchanting storybook that sparks curiosity and a love for learning. Her captivating storytelling weaves imagination with education, inspiring children to think critically and dream big. Her mission is to help parents, educators, and young readers unlock the power of literacy, creativity, and imagination while fostering a lifelong passion for knowledge. When she’s not writing or teaching, Shivani enjoys storytelling sessions, swimming, and connecting with families worldwide.

books.title: Osum’s Ocean Odyssey - Discovering Wonders and Friends Under the Sea
books.description: In the vibrant depths of the ocean, there lives a curious and wise octopus named Osum, who grew up listening to adventurous tales of exploration from his parents and grandparents. Determined to carve his own path, young Osum convinces his family to go on an exploration voyage all alone. As he navigate the colorful coral reefs, they encounter a shark in an old, colossal ship and discovered the hidden treasure. Along the way, Osum learns valuable lessons from his mistakes and finds joy in helping others. He discovers the importance of honesty, teamwork, and understanding one’s environment. Ultimately, Osum realizes that the true spirit of exploration lies not just in the discoveries made, but in the bonds forged along the way. Join Osum on his thrilling journey through the ocean’s depths, where adventure awaits at every turn!

books.title: Osum’s Ocean Odyssey - The Guardian of the Magical Seashell
books.description: Osum’s Ocean Odyssey- The Guardian of the Magical Seashell follows Osum, a curious young octopus who dreams of exploring the ocean and helping others. When he receives a mysterious seashell that hums with emotions, Osum discovers it reacts whenever the creatures around him are struggling inside. From anger and fear to joy and sadness, Osum learns that every emotion has a purpose. With humour, courage, and guidance from his wise Grandpa, he helps his ocean friends understand their feelings and express them safely, before the reef is overwhelmed by emotional chaos. A gentle, heartfelt underwater adventure about empathy, friendship, and learning that no emotion is wrong, only unheard.

books.title: Oops! I Messed Up Again!
books.description: Oops! I Messed Up Again! is a heartwarming story that teaches children how to embrace mistakes, overcome fears, build courage, and grow self-confidence, helping them understand emotions in a positive and encouraging way. Together, this children’s book combo supports reading habits, emotional learning, confidence building, creativity, empathy, and life skills development while keeping kids entertained with relatable and engaging stories.

fiction_catalogue.author: Uma Yoganand
fiction_catalogue.bio: Uma is a passionate writer who combines her corporate experience with a flair for creative storytelling. With more than 15 years in the corporate sector, including her role as General Manager at a BPO company in Mumbai, she brings a wealth of practical insight to her writing. Uma holds a Master’s degree in Statistics and a Postgraduate Diploma in Journalism, both earned in Bangalore, as well as an Executive MBA from MAHE. Her writing reflects a deep understanding of life’s complexities and is driven by a desire to inspire and empower women and youth to pursue their goals, overcome obstacles, and follow their dreams. Outside of writing, Uma enjoys yoga, hiking, and spending time with her family.

books.title: One Life and the Road
books.description: ‘Do I really know him’ asks Shruti, after having known Amit for a decade and married to him for four years. What is the truth about how Amit’s past affects their relationship today? What is at stake in their relationship? Does childhood trauma impact behavioral traits in adult life? As they walk the tightrope of realities, will they successfully overcome the challenges? Uma effortlessly draws their “emotional maps” from the patterns that emerge when hurt and when experiencing relationship problems. One Life and the road, also delves into the tenor of what love means, what acceptance feels like, how crucial is holistic upbringing in a child’s early life, etc.

books.title: One Life and the Betrayal
books.description: She gave him her heart. He asked for her soul. Shruti thought motherhood would bring her closer to Amit. Instead, it exposed the cracks in their marriage. As she pours herself into caring for their newborn daughter Aadita, Amit drifts away—into old wounds, new temptations, and a dangerous need for attention. When therapy fails and betrayal strikes, Shruti must make a choice: fight for a love that’s slipping away or protect the life she’s built for her child. One life and the betrayal is a powerful tale of love, loss, and rediscovery. It’s about the strength it takes to heal, the courage to let go, and the hope that every ending carries the seed of a new beginning.

books.title: Women of History
books.description: FROM THE SHORES OF ULLAL TO THE HEART OF THE DECCAN, A HISTORY WRITTEN IN FIRE. For too long, the legends of Karnataka have been dictated by conquerors, relegating the true history of valor to whispers. But historian Dr. Asha Rao is about to challenge the official record, her discovery of three hidden artifacts, a Portuguese battle dispatch, a scroll carrying an ancient ballad, and a powerful Mughal decree, opens a portal to three extraordinary lives that history was determined to forget. Step into the exhilarating, fictionalized lives of the Deccan's Lionesses: Rani Abbakka: The coastal queen who used brilliant guerrilla naval warfare to ensure the technologically superior Portuguese never claimed the spice ports of Belawadi Mallamma, the fearless warrior queen who, on horseback, faced down the elite forces of Chatrapati Shivaji, earning the Maratha leader's awe and respect through sheer, unyielding courage born of grief. Keladi Chennamma: The political master who risked total annihilation to uphold the code of honor, weaponizing the Malnad forests to outwit the mighty Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb when he demanded the surrender of a fugitive prince. Women of History is a novel of patriotism, fortitude, and strategic genius. It is a demand to reclaim the stories that prove the spirit of Indian freedom was born not in 1857, but centuries earlier, and it was championed by queens.

fiction_catalogue.author: Lakshmi Ajoy
fiction_catalogue.bio: Dr. Lakshmi Ajoy is a Bestselling Author, Magnetic Personality Coach and Holistic Spiritual Healer with an experience that spans 15 years. Apart from her work as a Life Coach where she conducts both online and offline sessions on topics relevant to physical, emotional and mental healing. She is also an expert in occult sciences and has acquired advanced certifications as an NLP (Neuro Linguistic Programming) Expert, PLR (Past Life Regression) and FLP (Future Life Progression) Therapist, Reiki Grand-master, Tarot Card Reader, Crystal Healer, Dowsing Therapist, Angel Healer, Sound Healer, Naturopath, Nature Healer, Chakra Healing, Advanced Numerologist, EFT (Emotional Freedom Technique) Practitioner, Vastu Consultant, Indian Flower Remedies and the likes. She has authored four solo books and over 25 anthologies; most of which have been best sellers on the Amazon list.

books.title: Untitled (Anuradhapura Portal)
books.description: An ancient relic. An advanced civilization. One move could alter their lives forever. When three teens stumble upon a portal hidden beneath the ruins of Anuradhapura, they awaken a timeline beyond their understanding... Buried beneath the sacred sands of Anuradhapura lies a truth that's long forgotten... but magically alive. When Ranpalee and her two best friends— Manil and Rajin— stumble upon a strange rock formation deep inside the ancient Ranmasu Uyana gardens, they simply know that it is more than just a piece of history. They explore and behold! The weathered carvings begin to glow. A thrumming energy pulls them in. Moments later, they're no longer in their world… or their time. Thrown into a realm beyond their understanding through the secret energy portal, the trio awakens in a dazzling space—one untouched by decay, illuminated by wisdom, ruled by knowledge, and hiding cosmic truths in perfect accordance with Nature.

books.title: The Secret Magic Lakes in Kelimutu
books.description: A hidden secret volcanic crater. Three magical lakes. One move could alter their lives forever. The Secret Magic Lakes in Kelimutu draws the readers into a realm where nature, mystery, spirituality and destiny converge in breathtaking harmony. Set against the awe-inspiring backdrop of Flores Island, this story traces the extraordinary journey of three inseparable friends— Ranpalee, Rajin, and Manil; whose lives take an unexpected turn when a strange calling from the tri-coloured crater lakes of Kelimutu begins to echo their curiosity. Known for their shifting hues and mystical legends, these lakes have fascinated explorers for centuries, but never before has a trio dared to pursue their secrets with such courage and purpose. Driven by curiosity and bound by an unshakeable bond, the three best friends embark on a quest that is far greater than a simple adventure. Each step they take across Flores’ rugged cliffs and deep lake bottoms draws them deeper into a world where spiritual forces and ancient energies breathe beneath the earth’s surface. But what truly awaits them at the heart of Kelimutu? Are they merely travellers, or chosen guardians of a destiny written long before their time? As they journey forward, questions linger in the air like whispers carried by the mountain winds: Will they succeed in their mission? Can they unlock the mysteries concealed within the ever-changing lakes? And more importantly, are they prepared for the truths that may reshape their understanding of reality itself? Their path is illuminated by friendship, challenged by unseen forces, and guided by the belief that every mystery holds a purpose. What lies beyond the veil of the ordinary is not just an adventure—it is a calling. After all, Kelimutu is not just a place of beauty and legend. It is a mystery magic portal, waiting to be unveiled.

fiction_catalogue.author: Jasbir Basu
fiction_catalogue.bio: Jasbir Basu is an award-winning Indian author and Chartered Accountant whose stories celebrate empathy, resilience and the quiet strength of the human heart. Guided by her mission to prove that compassion and kindness are the true forces that sustain humanity, she writes emotional short stories and poems that touch deeply and heal gently. Before turning to writing full-time, Jasbir worked in the credit sector for over a decade, blending analytical discipline with creative sensitivity. Her debut book, Not Without My Mother, won the India Authors Academy Award and established her as a powerful new voice in contemporary Indian literature. Her writing often explores emotional endurance, family relationships and spiritual awakening, reflecting her lifelong fascination with human nature and the unseen layers of existence. An ardent lover of cinema, spirituality and family-oriented stories, Jasbir draws inspiration from authors such as Ayn Rand, Agatha Christie and Khaled Hosseini — visionaries known for their powerful storytelling and timeless emotional depth. Like them, she seeks to create literature that lingers in the reader’s heart long after the last page is turned. Jasbir believes that stories are more than words — they are bridges between souls. Through her books, she hopes to build those bridges across generations and geographies, reminding readers everywhere that in a changing world, the heart remains the strongest compass.

books.title: THE LAST WISH
books.description: “The dead don’t always rest in peace... especially when love, guilt or secrets hold them back.” They appear in the quiet hours of the night — in mirrors, in dreams, in the places they once called home. Spirits torn from life too soon, bound by a final desire they cannot fulfil alone. Now, they’ve returned… to ask one last favour from those they left behind. In this gripping collection of paranormal short stories, each tale unravels a ghost’s final journey — a soldier begging for closure, a tormented child revealing the darkest truth, a father connecting with his dearest daughter to fulfil his dream. Every story is a chilling reminder that love can defy death — but so can regret. Thrilling, atmospheric and deeply human, THE LAST WISH explores the fragile line between this world and the next — and what happens when that line is crossed. Would you grant a final wish… even if it meant facing the dead?

books.title: Not Without My Mother
books.description: Dive into the heartfelt book "Not Without My Mother", a captivating collection of short stories that unveil the untold narratives of mothers and their extraordinary struggles. In this poignant compilation, each tale is a testament to the resilience, sacrifice and boundless love that defines the essence of motherhood. What are we without our mothers? The only unconditional form of love is that of a mother for her child. What happened to Radhika when she lost her baby? Read her story to find out how she dealt with her pain and how her life became a blessing.

books.title: The Invisible Thread
books.description: “The Invisible Thread” is a powerful collection of short stories which brings together ordinary people at the exact moments their lives could break - or bend towards hope. A flooded street in Mumbai. A desperate cry of a child. A promise kept when it would have been easier to walk away. Across these emotionally charged stories, strangers become saviours, compassion becomes courage and empathy quietly rewrites fate. There are no superheroes here - only people who choose to care when it matters most. From professionals juggling ambition and conscience to vulnerable souls searching for dignity, each story delivers a sharp emotional punch and a deeply satisfying close. Subtle connections weave the narratives together, reminding us that even brief encounters can leave permanent marks. Perfect for readers who love: 1) Heartfelt, character-driven stories 2) Uplifting yet realistic endings 3) Stories about humanity, resilience and hope 4) Short reads with lasting emotional impact. If you believe kindness can be revolutionary—and that small choices can create ripples far beyond their moment—this book belongs on your shelf.

fiction_catalogue.author: Pratima Kapoor
fiction_catalogue.bio: Pratima Kapur chanced upon writing in 2005. An artist by profession, Pratima wore many hats as an Army officer’s wife . . . a dramatist, choreographer, compère, interior designer, actor, model and a teacher. Writing, however, has been by far the most satisfying experience for her . . . to be able to express her feelings and relate her stories in her books and illustrate them, is a gift she will be ever thankful for. Living presently with her mother Kuntala Muzumdar and husband Lt Gen Rana Kapur, and their spaniel, Cookie, in Pune, India, she divides her time between her home in the city and their farm, which is a little bit of Paradise that brings her all the inspiration she needs for her creative work.

books.title: TAPESTRY
books.description: TAPESTRY – my first novel 2014. It is a story set in Pune, about four friends in modern India who have been fortunate to have lived life on their terms. They have reunited after decades but their plans to spend their sunset years together, have been disrupted by a horrific incident.

books.title: Untitled (Rajpura Generation Novel)
books.description: This is a story about four generations, set in Rajpura, near Dehradun. There is a wolf amongst the lambs but he is in disguise. Only one person has seen the wolf for who he truly is and plans to bring justice to the land. Will that person succeed?

books.title: Angelica Angel and the Pappalooza Choozas (Coloring Pack Combo)
books.description: Being the illustrator, I have converted the colour pictures from my storybook, Angelica Angel and the Pappalooza Choozas, into black and white and made a matching colouring book. For good measure, I created a bookmark and added a set of colour pencils. This is complete package for a child while travelling as well. A combination of a storybook with a matching colouring book is the first of its kind internationally.

books.title: Untitled (Army Kid Bombay Novel)
books.description: This is a book about a young girl whose father is an Army Officer and has been posted to Bombay. The young girl is whisked away at no notice and is unable to say goodbye to her friends and neighbours, making her angry and sad and unwilling to adjust to her new surroundings. She wishes for a special friend to help her cope with this trauma. Does she find this friend and does she finally accept her life in Bombay and find joy? This is a book of laughter and adventure for the children whilst touching upon a few values like kindness, love of animals and empathy.

books.title: Untitled (The Diary of Horror and Flames)
books.description: Groups of people silently filled the space in front of the entrance to their home, entering through the narrow gate. The tiny, delicate, twelve year old turned to see soldiers in the far distance. As she stood there watching them, the soldiers knelt down, picked up their rifles and took aim. The officers gave the command. “Fire” she heard, as her joy turned to horror, as a volley of bullets tore through the masses. In terror, she watched them take aim once again and shoot at the crowd . . . wave upon wave of metal that destroyed everything in their path. She stood by that wretched window, witnessing her entire life go up in flames. Decades later, Poornima, chanced upon the diary written by this young girl. . . words that fascinated influenced her and inspired her to create a new world for her family.

fiction_catalogue.author: Chetna Bhatia
fiction_catalogue.bio: Ms. Chetna Bhatia, a teacher, an art lover and a nature lover, born and brought up in Jamnagar Gujarat, now settled in Mumbai. She has been in the field of education for around 35 years. Her profession as a teacher developed a passion for writing in her. She has contributed several poems, quotes and stories to more than 50 National and International anthologies and also 2 solo quote books (bestsellers on Amazon) and 2 solo collections of poems. 1 book on short stories. An editor and compiler of 15 Gujarati anthologies, she writes in 3 different languages, English, Hindi and Gujarati and is working towards giving a platform to new writers and keeping the native language (Gujarati) alive.

books.title: The Silent Wisdom of Childhood
books.description: A heart-touching collection of stories inspired by real-life classroom experiences, capturing the emotional world of children. Each story provides subtle yet powerful insights into children's behaviour, offering parents and teachers a window into their inner worlds. Simple in narration, rich in message – this book is a gentle reminder to listen, observe, and understand. The silent wisdom of childhood has won the Best short stories Award.

books.title: Whimsical Tales of Whiskers and Wonders
books.description: Anthology - 8 short stories. Collection of grandma – grandpa stories. Whimsical Tales of Whiskers and Wonders, written by 8 authors each sharing a unique story exploring the theme Grandma Grandpa tales. This magical collection brings together stories full of wonder, adventure, kindness, and imagination.

books.title: In Their Tiny Shoes
books.description: In Their Tiny Shoes is a collection of five thoughtful stories that invite readers to see the world through a child’s eyes. Through simple moments, unexpected mistakes, and quiet realizations, these stories explore empathy, responsibility, and the power of understanding. Each tale gently reminds us that children often feel more than they can express—and sometimes, silence teaches the loudest lessons. A meaningful read for children, and a gentle wake-up call for adults.

fiction_catalogue.author: Story Teller Scientist (Nitesh Pandey)
fiction_catalogue.bio: Nitesh Pandey is a scientist and educator. He works in the area of Astrobiology and evolutionary medicine where he uses the principles of evolutionary biology to derive insights about life - both terrestrial and extraterrestrial. He is a passionate educator who believes in sharing the human side of science. He believes that the stories of the characters behind greatest of science discoveries must be told to the young generation and with this very spirit he wrote the 'The Curious Case of Dr. Pragyan Parker'. He writes under the pen name 'Storyteller Scientist'..

books.title: The Curious Case of Dr. Pragyan Parker
books.description: "Did you know there is a flower that intentionally tries to look and smell like rotting meat? Why would it adopt such a bizarre strategy? Imagine a family in Italy that wouldn't even know if they accidentally broke their legs... Dr. Pragyan Parker, a world-renowned scientist and educator, delves into these captivating scientific mysteries. However, his storytelling serves a dire purpose―to save his son, Rachit, from a kidnapper who desires not money but something far more valuable: the secrets of Dr. Parker's groundbreaking secret project, approved personally by the prime minister. In his desperate quest to rescue his only son, Dr. Parker faces harrowing moral dilemmas. Must he choose between his son and the future of his country? What do the kidnappers really want? Dive into this rollercoaster ride of a book to discover some of the most astonishing stories about science and the scientists behind them."

fiction_catalogue.author: Gautam Mayekar
fiction_catalogue.bio: Gautam Mayekar is a Security Architect and cyber‑thriller author with over a decade of experience in IT and cybersecurity, having worked with organisations like John Deere, Tibco, Quick Heal, Qualys, and eQ Technologic. As an author, he is best known for his “SoHacked” series, blending technical authenticity with fast-paced, cinematic storytelling rooted in real-world cyber concepts. He has also served as a judge for Kavach, the Ministry of Home Affairs’ national cybersecurity hackathon, recognising his expertise and contributions to India’s cybersecurity ecosystem.

books.title: Chakravyuh (SoHacked Series)
books.description: Rudra, an ethical hacker, Lily, a cyber-crime lawyer and Mohseen, a journalist, are all by night a part of ICD – Indian Cyber Defence, an underground hacking forum that defaces Pakistani Government websites and spreads viruses to attack them. @shok, introducing himself as a RAW agent, joins the forum, and makes an irresistible offer – crack the puzzle of Chakravyuh to get a chance to do something truly worthwhile to serve the nation. Cracking various stages of Chakravyuh, they are lead deeper and deeper - into now developing cyber-weapons for a great war. Fighting their personal demons, dealing with confusion & rebellion within the group, they race against time to hack their next target – CRAWL, a secret division of RAW itself. But what could be the motive? Who is @shok really? What is he leading them into? Are they really serving the nation or is there something more sinister going on?

books.title: @v! (SoHacked Series)
books.description: @v! is a hacker who spends his days working in India’s number one anti-virus company as a security researcher and his nights, crawling through underground hacking forums. He is completely unmotivated and lacks purpose until he receives a letter from M@dR1; a mysterious revolutionary who wants to create a “change” in the world. M@dR1 uses @v!’s horrific past to motivate him, and guides him through various tasks, starting with shutting down the internet services of the entire city. A series of mayhem, manipulation and mystery ensues as @v! begins hallucinating various events and people from his former life, before he became a reclusive hacker. He is thrown into a barrage of uncertainty and confusion as his perception of reality begins to shift out of focus. Meanwhile, M@dR1 and @v! prepare for a major cyber- attack that will rock the foundation of the country’s technological unit, with the help of @v!’s friends, $@m and K!LL3R. Will they accomplish their goals? Or will @v!’s increasing delusion lead to their downfall?

books.title: Untitled (CO-Weed-20 Time Machine Thriller)
books.description: Amar- the man from the future, is narrating his story into the Voice Data Recorder of Time machine through which he has travelled back in time. He is alone & stuck in the machine with no expertise or knowledge to operate it. With Mohit – the one who built this Time Machine, having tested CO-Weed-20 positive and gone to sleep permanently, just like the entire population of Mumbai city; Amar is left only with a stash of weed and wait of twelve hours until the machine disintegrates. Before it does, Amar will reveal the secrets behind the production of the deadly drug – CO-Weed-20 and how it all went awry. Who is Amar? Will he survive & eventually save the world? Or will he get beaten by the mysterious Thakur from his dreams again?

fiction_catalogue.author: Deepti Kolte
fiction_catalogue.bio: Deepti Kolte believes in the transformative power of poetry and her writings are characterized by their simplicity and profundity. Her second poetry book, 'The Light at the Edges, has received accolades from eminent author Hansda Sowvendra Shekhar. Her poems have also appeared in notable anthologies and international journals. Recently, she published her third book, 'Unfinished Love,' which is a collection of seven mini stories. Alongside her literary pursuits, she is a marketing professional and promotes mental health awareness through her top- ranking podcast, Mental Health Musings. 'The Light at the Edges' has won Sahitya Sparsh Award 2024 for Poetry/Self-reflection and LiFT Award 2024 as Editor's Pick along with first rank.

books.title: The Light at the Edges
books.description: ’The Light at the Edges’ is a collection of poems that celebrates the small joys in the ordinary moments of life and exudes hope. The poems subtly convey that hope can be found in such ways and it can show us the light we all need. The poetry book is sure to leave you with a warm and refreshing sense of optimism. The book also has a space for the reader to reflect and write out their little moments of delight. What sets the poems apart is their accessibility and how effortlessly they connect with our everyday familiar experiences. The book has been resonating with a wide range of readers, including those new to poetry. The simple yet powerful thoughts written alongside each poem help unwrap the essence of the poem.

books.title: Unfinished Love
books.description: Seven fictional mini stories of self-discovery that transcends the heartache of unfinished love. This is a book to be savoured, not hurried through. These are stories not with an intriguing plot or traditional narrative, but with words that guide you through moments of self-discovery shaped by love. From spontaneous walks and moonlit conversations to the warmth of a shared silence, these stories capture those fragile, fleeting moments when love simply is, unbounded by expectations or endings. For anyone who has ever loved deeply, lost quietly, and found beauty in the in-between, this book will speak to the love in your heart.

fiction_catalogue.author: Ashwini R Sane
fiction_catalogue.bio: Ashwini R Sane is a poet and an author who writes in the genre of science fiction fantasy mythology. Her poems have also come in anthologies to name a few; CITYSCAPES: an Anthology of Poetry, Unkahi – Buffering Beyond the Barrel and AVANI- Army Wives Welfare Association of India. She has studied abroad and has been exposed to varied communities, cultures and languages. She is married to a doctor in the army and has one son. She enjoys music, dabbles in painting landscapes, and loves nature and animals. She also loves to play tennis and wishes to continue playing till the ripe old age of eighty. Contact her at @saneashwini on instagram Social Media : https://linktr.ee/ashwinirsane.

books.title: The Sonic Reverberator – the origins of the Kanya
books.description: A little girl The Little Kanya is created by the Gods to find the celestial weapons of the Goddess, manifest the Devi and vanquish the Asur who wants to vanquish the Goddess, take those powerful weapons and conquer the world. The Little Kanya Isha is helped by the A-tithi’s those who lead eternal lives and the three Queens of Antiquity who vow to help the Little Kanya in all her adventures. The stories take you from the realms of outer space, to the hot sands of ancient Persia, to a school in Kolkata and adventures through different countries, solving different puzzles, encountering strange creatures.

books.title: The Moscow Mosaic
books.description: The story moves to search for the celestial weapons and objects in Moscow.

books.title: The Vienna Juggernaut
books.description: The story is concluded in Vienna.

books.title: Untitled (Post-Apocalyptic Ushtakas Sky Saga)
books.description: In ice cold winter there is a disease in a futuristic post- apocalyptic world. Death is a common occurrence due to a strange disease. On earth the abandoned clan of the Ushtakas have an ‘Ajobi’ girl who does not look like them. She, her wolf, her snake and her best friend escape to the sky to the which they believe is the ‘Abodes of the Gods’ where they shall reach the Heavens of Gold. But heavens have their own hell. She does not know. Who is she? What is she? Will she survive death? The readers will follow her journey to know what is in the Abodes of the Gods . . . Paradise or Hell?

books.title: Untitled (Kashmir Deployment Dialogue)
books.description: Written based on a true story of my spouse being deployed in Kashmir and the death of his tent mate in a search and rescue operation in the Kashmir Valley. It is a drama dialogue between a soldier, a terrorist and the hostage a woman. They all have their own dilemmas and justifications of their positions; one for the country, the other for his ideals, the woman for her point of view and status in society. Join these characters to know who is the winner, who is the loser and what is the solution?

fiction_catalogue.author: Amita Samant
fiction_catalogue.bio: Amita’s writing journey commenced with her blog – Candid Thoughts where she offers a medley of experiences, opinions, fiction, and poetry. Her fascination for the magical worlds sparked her interest in children’s fiction. So, armed with some crazy imagination and a fondness for all creatures with four legs, wings, or tails, Amita began spinning some magic of her own. She freelanced for a children's newspaper and her stories and poems have also been published on websites and in anthologies. Amita holds a Masters in Economics and has worked with research organizations before venturing into her true calling. Apart from writing, she loves sketching, painting, and crafts.

books.title: Untitled (Award-Winning Children's Storybook)
books.description: The book features five stories, each framed with a unique narrative, set in imaginative surroundings, and sprinkled with engaging dialogue. It is ideal for 8 to 12-year-olds but is enjoyable for teenagers as well. The stories are mostly fun reads, but values such as positivity, compassion, and self-love are organically woven in. By igniting imagination and fuelling curiosity, the book is a perfect escape from screens. It is a great gift to foster a love of reading among young ones. Award: The book has won the Authoropod Magazine’s 2025 Award for Best Children’s Literature.

books.title: Who Shall Wear the Crown
books.description: The crayons of Art Valley are facing a dilemma. They wish to crown the most deserving colour as their King. So, each colour flaunts its exquisite qualities to prove its superiority over the rest. The argument escalates into an ugly fight. How will the dispute be resolved? An unexpected turn of events brings the answer. ‘Who Shall Wear the Crown’ showcases the power of unity through a cheerful poem and eye-catching images. The book highlights how our unique talents reach new heights when we complement each other. It is ideal for early readers or apt for parents to read out to their young ones.

fiction_catalogue.author: Nima Mankar
fiction_catalogue.bio: Nima Ashok Mankar is a retired Primary School teacher. She has worked as an English teacher in Baroda High School, Vadodara (Gujarat) for twenty-five years. Her educational qualifications are, M.A. (English Literature), B.Ed, Diploma in Computer Hardware and Networking (Jetking), A certificate in Journalism (B.I.E.T., Mumbai). She has been on the Editorial Team of the School Magazine. She has worked as a content writer. She has more than 2.5K followers on social media. She has published a Novel: Reaping Rewards - a series of sweet, successful stories, a novella, “Dear You … … Yours Truly, Kavita” and five coffee table books.

books.title: Dear You … … Yours Truly, Kavita
books.description: Kavita is a simple girl, whose life has been full of hardships. Taking misfortunes in her stride, she smoothly and successfully sails through life. She lives with the memories of a childhood heartthrob. Beauty is in remembering! She keeps encountering Rajat at various phases of her life. But, do they finally unite forever? Find out. Excerpt: I heard a deep, masculine voice quietly exclaim, "Hey, shutter-bug!" I ignored it. Obviously, that wasn't for me. There were so many photographers around. Blissfully, I continued capturing beautiful and rare photos of celebrities. I was so occupied, that I didn't realize that I was using somebody else's camera to do so!

fiction_catalogue.author: Reyanshi Chouhan
fiction_catalogue.bio: Reyanshi Chouhan is a student of class 9th. She is the youngest author in our group and has penned a wonderful fiction novel, that will leave you spell bound by her imagination and writing skills and command over the language as well.

books.title: Untitled (Guardian of the Infinite Triangle)
books.description: The city choked, a pressure cooker ready to blow... Urgent news flashes blared from screens... Headlines screamed: "Anonymous Killer Claims 30 Lives! Murderer Lurks, No One Safe!" Into this swirling mess stumbled Aelin, a quiet city girl who preferred dusty books to heroic quests and her own company to crowded parties. She was the type who could vanish into a crowd, a ghost in the city's winding streets. Then, her world exploded. She discovered, with the force of a magical earthquake, that she was the unexpected guardian of the Infinite Triangle, the multiverse's most powerful artifact. This monstrous responsibility, like a poorly wrapped, ticking time bomb, shoved her into a deadly game – a maze of ancient prophecies, hidden powers, and dangers you couldn't even imagine. Suddenly, Aelin, the unassuming city girl, stood on the edge of oblivion.

books.title: Untitled (Ari Tamang and Divine Inheritance)
books.description: At St. Jude's, Ari Tamang was the star point guard with a drum-kit-fueled temper-until he accidentally turned his Honors English classroom into a giant pile of floor-glitter. Surprise: his "anger issues" are actually a divine inheritance from Shiva, the Destroyer. Now, instead of worrying about the playoffs, he's discovering that the universe has a "delete" button, and he's the one with his finger on it. To keep reality from being unmade, Ari has to team up with Edward, a legacy of Vishnu whom he's spent years pranking, and Genesis, Brahma-powered artist he used to tease. It's basically the world's most awkward group project: the popular bully and his favorite targets have to synchronize their "Create, Preserve, and Trash" powers before the cosmos gets factory-reset.

fiction_catalogue.author: Divya Chakraborty
fiction_catalogue.bio: Meet Divya Chakraborty, a Pune-based Entrepreneur and Passionate Author. Renowned for crafting deeply emotional and immersive stories, Divya captivates readers with heartfelt emotions and compelling characters. Her debut novel, Bound by Unconditional Love, a tribute to military wives, resonated with readers worldwide and has gained recognition from a production house, showcasing its cinematic potential. Building on this success, Divya now presents another compelling saga that delves deep into women’s inner battles, their enduring strength, and the light within that guides them through life’s darkest trials. With a loyal global fan base spanning over 10 countries, Divya has established herself as a standout author in the digital era of e-novels. Her writing beautifully captures the essence of love, painting vivid images of connections and encounters that linger in readers’ hearts long after the last page.

books.title: Bound by Unconditional Love
books.description: In the depths of her despair, Avani's voice shattered the silence, "I hate you, Major Rishabh Malhotra! I hate you! You broke my trust; you broke me today. I will never forgive you.” Avani, a compassionate 25-year-old Financial Analyst, saw her world crumble when Rishabh Malhotra, a 27-year-old Army Major, broke her heart as he went off to a mission with zero chances of returning unscathed, leaving her heartbroken and shattered. But fate had other plans in store. What unfolds, when Rishabh endeavours to mend the love he once broke, all while fulfilling his duty? Can Avani's shattered heart ever be made whole again, especially when the truth comes to light? Will they find their way back to happiness or are some wounds too deep to heal? This is a story of love, heartbreak, and the quest for redemption, where two fractured hearts must find a way to mend the irreparable.

books.title: Beneath Her Silence
books.description: Aahi Parker, an entrepreneur—a fierce, resilient woman who built her success from the ground up. Confident and determined, but behind her unyielding demeanour lies a silence a truth that no one knows. Siddharth Singh, the enigmatic and commanding CEO of Singh Empire, sees beyond Aahi's composed exterior. He sees the cracks in her armor, a secret she guards fiercely. While he's willing to risk everything to stand by her, Aahi walks a perilous path driven by power, vengeance, or justice. What drives her relentless pursuit? As the past and present collide, Aahi must decide whether the strength she has built is enough to face the truth and embrace a future that might finally set her free and Siddharth needs to go beyond and recognize what lies Beneath Her Silence!!!

fiction_catalogue.author: Nisha Shenoy
fiction_catalogue.bio: Nisha Shenoy is an Amazon Bestselling Author, 6 published books, TEDx speaker, Podcaster, Asia Book of Records holder, and Founder of TVĀM: Inner Revolution Hub focused on building Inner Mastery over Outward Success, a learning platform helping individuals discover their authentic self. A CA-turned-HR Leader with over 18 years in Corporate Leadership across Operations, Training & HR before choosing a purpose-led path in Storytelling, Emotional Mastery & Embodied Consciousness.

books.title: Untitled (Itika Benegal Corporate Law Novel)
books.description: What if the very thing you were taught to hide became the reason you were chosen? Set against the sharp edges of a high-powered corporate law firm, this novel dives into the internal world of Itika Benegal — a brilliant, guarded, asexual woman navigating ambition, identity, and the politics of power. Recruited by the country’s top law firm under suspicious circumstances, Itika finds herself caught between two formidable giants, Ayesha Dhar and Sunny Nair, locked in a silent war. But as boardroom rivalries intensify, a devastating personal revelation shatters the foundation of everything she thought she knew about herself and her family. This is not merely a corporate drama. It is an intimate exploration of invisibility, desire, identity, and the quiet burden of being “different” in a world obsessed with labels and longing. As ambition collides with desire, Itika must confront a forbidden path that could either redefine her strength—or leave a permanent scar. A story about power, secrecy, and self-acceptance, this book unpacks what it truly means to own your flaw — before it owns you.

books.title: The Friday Girl
books.description: When a buried family secret fractures her carefully constructed world, Itika Benegal is forced into an impossible choice: cling to the identity she built in the present—or walk back into a past her mother fought hard to erase. Set between the restless pulse of Mumbai and the mystic forests of Dandeli, this time-bending romantic suspense unravels the inner lives of two women on the edge of ruin—a daughter searching for belonging, and a mother haunted by choices she cannot undo. At its core, this is a story across two generations about identity in transition. About the kind of love that heals—and the kind that wounds. About spiritual awakening that doesn’t arrive gently, but through collapse, longing, and the final unravelling of truth. As ambition, ancestry, romance, and faith come colliding together, Itika must confront the question we all secretly fear: Are you willing to let go of who you are to become the one you were meant to be? Some journeys don’t lead you forward or backward. They take you inward. Brace for impact.

fiction_catalogue.author: Bindu Unnikrishnan
fiction_catalogue.bio: Bindu Unnikrishnan is an acclaimed author, poet, educator, and marathoner with over two decades of teaching experience. Author of My Treasure Trove of Emotions and Luminara – Poetry That Touches the Soul, she has co-authored more than fifty anthologies. A winner of several national poetry and creative writing competitions and crowned Mrs. Maharashtra 2023 (Elite), she continues to inspire through her words and work.

books.title: SONARELLE
books.description: What if a single moment could follow you forever? SONARELLE is an evocative collection of thirty stories where memories arrive unannounced, time folds in on itself, and ordinary lives crack open to reveal extraordinary secrets. Moving fluidly between the real and the uncanny, these globally resonant tales explore love, loss, forgotten dreams, and the quiet terror of the lives we almost lived. Haunting, intimate, and impossible to forget, SONARELLE is a book that listens back-long after the last page is turned.

fiction_catalogue.author: Diya Santhosh
fiction_catalogue.bio: Diya Santhosh is a graphic designer in her final year at Kingston University, whose practice explores the relationship between type, narrative, and image. Her approach is rooted in making as a form of dialogue—between theory and practice, experimentation and understanding—resulting in work that balances concept with material exploration. Diya draws inspiration from her experiences, travels, and the people she encounters. Her work often centers on storytelling and the beauty of individuality. An avid reader, trekking enthusiast, poetry lover, and dedicated journaler, she is also an author and illustrator, having published her first children’s book Iris? Iris, a project that reflects her interest in playful, narrative-driven design. Diya Santhosh has wanted to illustrate and write a book her whole life and over the pandemic she put together “Iris? Iris” - a short wholesome read.

books.title: Iris? Iris
books.description: The writing is inspired by her own journals as a child and it was illustrated during the pandemic. It is a book about self-discovery and things we’re all familiar with but sometimes need to be reminded about. There is no age group - it is dedicated to everyone :)

fiction_catalogue.author: Shefali Arora
fiction_catalogue.bio: An Assistant Professor, Computer Science in NIT Jalandhar, by profession and author by passion. Her work blends rigorous research in the field of AI, while exploring the human stories and weaving characters.

books.title: Untitled (Alunkrita and Anokhi's Startup Story)
books.description: When you land on the crossroads of life, all you need is the courage and attitude to tread on this path covered with obstacles. For there is always a turn which basks in the glory of sunshine… Alunkrita can go to any extent to get Anokhi’s, the disabled girl she found on the streets of Chandigarh, life sorted. Her start-up idea holds the key to all her dreams and Anokhi’s bright future. She plans to send Anokhi to a boarding school once her feet get operated. Parth, who broke her heart years ago, offers her the most appropriate deal for the start-up. Battling between her past and her growing fondness for her new neighbour, Siddarth, would Alunkrita make the right choices for her life and, most importantly, for Anokhi? Would Anokhi’s future ever see the light of dawn?

fiction_catalogue.author: Siddharth Sen
fiction_catalogue.bio: Dr. Siddharth Sen is an Asia and India Book of Records holder and prolific author of 41+ published books, including 30+ bestsellers. He lives in Kolkata and is fondly called Mr. Bookstore as his books are both in Fiction and Non-Fiction category of various genres from Thriller, Romance, Horror, Poetry, Self Help, Comedy and many more. His debut English novel, Adventures of Sami, earned significant critical acclaim and has been translated into Malayalam, Spanish, German, and Chinese, other popular ones include The Manor, The Girl In The Well, Kya Yehi Pyaar Hai, and Don’t Overthink, A Quote A Day and more. A former Fortune 500 Business Consultant with a two-decade corporate career and 400+ travels, Siddharth now leads a global community rooted in the unwavering belief that "Everything is Possible".

books.title: The Manor
books.description: Many times, we experience something unexpected—a narrow escape from a deadly accident because we were late and missed the bus or train we were supposed to board. Have you ever felt that someone might have intervened to save you? We often dismiss these incidents as mere luck, but is luck truly the answer, or is it a reflection of a deeper, ongoing battle between good and evil? Raj and Karishma might be unaware of the gravity of the decisions they've been drawn into, but will destiny offer them a second chance? In the heart of a forsaken forest, shrouded in perpetual mist, stands the village of Amtya, home to an ancient manor that defies the constrictions of time and sanity. Its weathered stones, etched with cryptic symbols, rise like the remnants of a forgotten nightmare. This is no ordinary house; it is a living entity, a pitcher of darkness that feeds on the fears and secrets of those who dare to cross its threshold. Will Raj and Karishma uncover the manor's sinister secrets? Grab your copy if you dare to read and unveil this deadly haunting tale.

fiction_catalogue.author: Vishwa Ratan
fiction_catalogue.bio: Dr. Vishwa Ratan is an Eye Specialist as well as a Public Health Specialist. He is wanderlust by nature and has travelled to many countries, working in different hospitals. His previous books include: A Doctor's Journey- A collection of short stories, A Night in the emergency room, and Pandemic stories around the World, are popular among readers.

books.title: Untitled (Baltic Solitude Separation)
books.description: Follow the journey of a young Indian couple who traveled to Poland for employment at an IT company. Due to a minor misunderstanding, the wife decides to make a significant choice to separate from her husband and explore other countries to reflect in solitude on the future of their relationship. Witness the gradual unraveling of the story as you accompany her to the Baltic nations and experience the journey until its conclusion.

fiction_catalogue.author: Neeti Paul
fiction_catalogue.bio: Neeti Paul is a certified Energy Healer, Hypnotherapy practitioner, and Best-selling Author - passionate about helping people heal from the inside out. With over 18 years of experience in energy healing, Neeti empowers readers to release emotional baggage, rediscover self-worth, and create lasting transformation in their lives. Drawing from her personal journey of overcoming hormonal imbalances, emotional eating, and self-doubt, and several challenges through her life, Neeti’s writing is infused with deep empathy, spiritual insight, and practical tools that truly work. Having worked as a hospitality professional for over 10 years has helped her understand the struggles of a working professional and a working mother. Neeti’s mission is to simplify healing and make it accessible to every house. Through her social media presence, she inspires conscious living, mindset shift, and self-love.

books.title: Untitled (Captain Akash & Tia Rescue Mission)
books.description: A routine rescue mission turns into something far more dangerous the moment Captain Akash finds an innocent girl named Tia trapped on the deserted terrace of a university. Akash is a man of restraint, discipline, and immaculate control—until one reckless moment changes everything. Their connection is instant. Intense. Undeniable. What begins as attraction soon spirals into a dangerous game of missing identities, buried truths, and powerful forces working in the shadows. When Tia vanishes without a trace, Akash is forced to question everything he believed. As loyalties are tested and the past resurfaces with deadly consequences, both Tia and Akash are pushed to their limits. Trust becomes fragile. Time starts running out. And every choice carries a price. Will Akash uncover the truth before love becomes a liability? Will Tia’s secrets cost them everything? Will love save them—or destroy them? If you love romantic thrillers packed with twists at every turn, this book is impossible to put down.

fiction_catalogue.author: Madhuri Maitra
fiction_catalogue.bio: Madhuri Maitra's world view is largely informed by her experiences as an army kid and an army wife. She has been a teacher, corporate trainer and writer, and now leads a slower life engaging with family, friends and simple community -enriching activities. She continues to write poetry and non- fiction.

books.title: Equinox
books.description: When Indus Publishers announces a short story competition, it affects the lives of the ensemble cast in unexpected ways. A jaded journalist, a bored housewife, a starry-eyed ambitious girl, an army colonel, an impoverished divorcee-all enter the competition for pressing reasons of their own. They emerge with only slightly deeper pockets than they had but far richer in experience. Social issues are explored in an engaging manner, entwined in the lives of the characters-this is indeed the way of life. The novel also promises an enchanting look at the diversity in India; the characters belong to different Indian states and embody the peculiarities of the people of that region. Equinox and its checkered characters step to music of their own; many readers will find that it resonates with their own inner music.
